title: Lily Yuri Tsurumaki Oral History
creator: Tessaku
description: See this link for the most complete and recent information on this record: https://repository.californiarevealed.org/node/454998 .

Oral history with Lily Yuri Tsurumaki (Oki) who was a child living in Los Angeles before the war. The Oki family was sent to the concentration camp in Heart Mountain, Wyoming during the war, and were given an opportunity to leave camp and farm in Utah for a brief period until the war ended. The family moved back to their original home in Los Angeles, where Lily first married Clarence Matsumura, a 442nd Regimental Combat Team veteran. Nearly 120,000 Japanese Americans were uprooted from their West Coast homes with the signing of Executive Order 9066 in February of 1942 and sent to inland concentration camps for four to five years.
